details: The "Leather Training Scheme" was started by the Department of Industries, Government of Rajasthan in the year 1990 with the aim of promoting the leather industry, improving the production and quality of leather products, developing new designs, and generating self-employment. Under this scheme, training in the latest techniques for leather dyeing, Nagara Jooti, leather goods, and leather toys is provided through District Industry Centers. The duration of the training is 2 months. Under the training program, a batch of 15 trainees has been designated for leather dyeing and Nagara Jooti making, while a batch of 20 trainees has been designated for leather goods and leather toy manufacturing.
benefits: Modern technical training in Nagara Jooti (15 trainees per batch).
Leather goods training (20 trainees per batch).
Each trainee receives ₹500/- per month during the training period.
Tool kits worth up to ₹1,000/- are provided to each trainee after successful completion of the training.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The applicant's age should be between 18 to 35 years.
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC), Scheduled Tribe (ST), Other Backward Class (OBC), or General category.
The Selection Committee has the authority to provide up to 10 years of age relaxation on a case-by-case basis.
Preference will be given to trainees who have passed 8th standard and are more educated.

offline: The applicant must write an application on plain paper mentioning complete personal details.
Attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the General Manager of the concerned District Industries and Commerce Centre (DIC).

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the General Manager to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission and a unique identification number (if applicable).

faqs.question: What is the duration of training under this scheme?
Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants aged 18–35 years from SC, ST, OBC, or General categories can apply.

faqs.question: Is there any age relaxation for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, up to 10 years of age relaxation can be given by the Selection Committee.

faqs.question: Do I need to be educated to apply?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be literate. Preference is given to those who have passed class 8 th or higher.

faqs.question: How much stipend is provided during training?
faqs.answer: ₹500 per month is provided as a stipend.

faqs.question: Where is the training conducted?
faqs.answer: At District Industries and Commerce Centres (DICs) across Rajasthan.

faqs.question: What is the batch size for each training type?
faqs.answer: 15 trainees for Nagara Jooti and 20 for Leather Goods.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Submit a handwritten application with full details to the District Industries Centre.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, there is no application fee.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to submit with my application?
faqs.answer: Age proof, caste certificate, educational proof, domicile, and ID proof.
